title: 30-Day Apigee X Training
subtitle: API proxy development for banking & UK Open Banking (FAPI)
author: Implementation / Customer Engineering

weeks.title: Week 1 — Foundations & Your First Proxy
weeks.theme: Understand the platform, get a working org, ship a proxy.

weeks.title: Week 2 — Core Proxy Development & Mediation
weeks.theme: The policy toolkit that does the real work in a proxy.

weeks.title: Week 3 — Security & Identity (through FAPI)
weeks.theme: Keys, OAuth, JWT, transport security, threats, and the FAPI profile.

weeks.title: Week 4 — UK Open Banking Domain
weeks.theme: Consent, AISP, PISP, DCR — built on Apigee X with FAPI security.

weeks.title: Week 5 — Operations, Delivery & Production
weeks.theme: Environments, CI/CD, observability, products, and going live.

1.title: Apigee X: the platform & architecture
1.objective: Explain what Apigee X is, how the control plane and runtime split, and how orgs, environments, and environment groups fit together.

2.title: Provision Apigee X & your toolchain
2.objective: Stand up a working Apigee X evaluation org on GCP and install the CLI tools you will use for the next 28 days.

3.title: Build & deploy your first API proxy
3.objective: Create, deploy, and call a reverse-proxy that fronts a backend target — and read the bundle that defines it.

4.title: Flows, conditions & routing
4.objective: Control which logic runs when, using PreFlow/PostFlow, conditional flows, and RouteRules.

5.title: Policies 101 & the execution model
5.objective: Attach your first policies and predict exactly when each one runs in the request/response pipeline.

6.title: Traffic management: quotas & rate limiting
6.objective: Protect backends with Quota, Spike Arrest, and Concurrent Rate Limit — and know which to use when.

7.title: Mediation & variables: AssignMessage + ExtractVariables
7.objective: Read, build, and reshape requests/responses using flow variables, AssignMessage, and ExtractVariables.

8.title: Transformation with JavaScript & message templates
8.objective: Apply custom logic and dynamic content with the JavaScript policy and message templating.

9.title: Service composition: ServiceCallout & target servers
9.objective: Call secondary services mid-flow and decouple backend hostnames with target servers.

10.title: Caching: response, lookup/populate & KVM
10.objective: Cut latency and backend load with ResponseCache and LookupCache/PopulateCache, and store config in KVMs.

11.title: Fault handling & error taxonomy
11.objective: Design predictable errors with RaiseFault, FaultRules, and DefaultFaultRule.

12.title: Shared flows, flow hooks & reuse (Week 2 capstone)
12.objective: Extract cross-cutting logic into shared flows and attach it everywhere with flow hooks.

13.title: App identity: API keys, products, apps & developers
13.objective: Model who can call your APIs using API products, developer apps, and VerifyAPIKey.

14.title: OAuth 2.0: client credentials & token endpoints
14.objective: Issue and verify OAuth tokens on Apigee with the OAuthV2 policy and the client-credentials grant.

15.title: OAuth authorization code + PKCE & JWT
15.objective: Implement the auth-code+PKCE flow and mint/verify JWTs for stateless, signed identity.

16.title: Transport security: TLS, mTLS & keystores
16.objective: Secure both edges of the proxy with one-way and mutual TLS using keystores and truststores.

17.title: Threat protection, CORS & data masking
17.objective: Block malformed and malicious payloads, enable CORS safely, and mask sensitive data in traces.

18.title: FAPI 1.0 Advanced: the security profile
18.objective: Read the FAPI 1.0 Advanced profile and map each requirement to a concrete Apigee mechanism.

19.title: Implementing FAPI on Apigee (Week 3 capstone)
19.objective: Build a FAPI-compliant authorization and resource flow: PAR, mTLS-bound tokens, and request objects.

20.title: UK Open Banking landscape & trust framework
20.objective: Map the OBIE ecosystem: roles (ASPSP/AISP/PISP), the Directory, and eIDAS/OB certificates.

21.title: Consent: the account-access consent model
21.objective: Build the consent lifecycle — create, authorize, and enforce an account-access consent.

22.title: AISP: account information APIs
22.objective: Expose Accounts, Balances, and Transactions endpoints that enforce consent and FAPI headers.

23.title: PISP: payment initiation APIs
23.objective: Implement domestic payment consent and payment submission with idempotency and funds confirmation.

24.title: Dynamic Client Registration, SSA & cert-bound tokens
24.objective: Onboard TPPs automatically by validating Software Statements and binding tokens to client certificates.

25.title: End-to-end Open Banking reference build (capstone)
25.objective: Assemble consent + AISP + PISP into one coherent, FAPI-secured Open Banking surface.

26.title: Environments, env groups, revisions & config
26.objective: Promote a proxy across environments cleanly using env groups, revisions, KVMs, and property sets.

27.title: CI/CD & config-as-code with apigeecli
27.objective: Automate build, test, and deploy of proxies through a GitHub Actions pipeline.

28.title: Observability: analytics, logging, tracing & debug
28.objective: See what your APIs are doing with Analytics, Cloud Logging, distributed tracing, and Debug sessions.

29.title: API products, portal, monetization & API hub
29.objective: Package, publish, govern, and (optionally) monetize APIs for the developers who consume them.

30.title: Production readiness & Open Banking capstone
30.objective: Run a go-live checklist — performance, security hardening, multi-region, and runbooks — over your OB build.
